accountPosts.name: Dario Amodei
accountPosts.text: Cyber is the first clear and present danger from frontier AI models, but it won’t be the last. If we are able to collectively rise to the challenge and confront this risk, it could serve as a blueprint for addressing the even more difficult challenges that lie ahead of us.

accountPosts.name: Dario Amodei
accountPosts.text: The dangers of getting this wrong are obvious, but if we get it right, there is a real opportunity to create a fundamentally more secure internet and world than we had before the advent of AI-powered cyber capabilities.

accountPosts.name: Dario Amodei
accountPosts.text: Glasswing is just the first step: patching and securing the world’s software infrastructure will be the work of months and years, and will require even broader cooperation across AI companies, cyberdefenders, software providers, governments, and more.

accountPosts.name: Dario Amodei
accountPosts.text: Rather than release Mythos Preview to general availability, we’re giving defenders early controlled access in order to find and patch vulnerabilities before Mythos-class models proliferate across the ecosystem.

accountPosts.name: Dario Amodei
accountPosts.text: We’ve been tracking the increasing cyber capabilities of AI models for years, which arise as part of their general proficiency at coding. But our new model, Mythos Preview, represents a particularly large step up.

accountPosts.name: Dario Amodei
accountPosts.text:
I’m proud that so many of the world’s leading companies have joined us for Project Glasswing to confront the cyber threat posed by increasingly capable AI systems head-on.

accountPosts.name: Jan Leike
accountPosts.text: When I started to work on the alignment problem more than 10 years ago, we had no idea how AGI was going to be built or how to make it safe. The field had maybe a dozen people who were working on it as a side gig. Everyone was pretty confused about how to approach the problem,

accountPosts.name: Jan Leike
accountPosts.text:
While a lot of progress has been made, I don’t think alignment is solved:

We still haven’t figured out how to supervise superhuman models and the stakes keep getting higher.

https://t.co/Ryc39GidJB

accountPosts.name: Jan Leike
accountPosts.text: To focus on this, I’ve stepped away from running alignment at Anthropic. @EthanJPerez and @sprice354_ are leading the team going forward, and I’m confident they’ll do an amazing job.

accountPosts.name: Jan Leike
accountPosts.text:
Some personal news: I am starting a new research project at Anthropic. Very excited about this!

Many things are needed to make AGI go well, and alignment is only one of them. More on this soon…

accountPosts.name: Jan Leike

For more details, check out the blog post 👇
https://t.co/62UUoPgxa1

accountPosts.name: Jan Leike
accountPosts.text:
However, most alignment research is not very crisp and requires research taste when evaluating.

This is why we chose to point the AAR at this scalable oversight problem! Progress would let AARs work on fuzzier alignment problems, where humans can only provide weak supervision.

accountPosts.name: Jan Leike
accountPosts.text: These AARs can also be applied to other alignment research projects that are “crisp”, i.e. where we can procedurally verify the quality of the work: automated red teaming, auditing games, control methods, etc.

accountPosts.name: Jan Leike
accountPosts.text: Moreover, even in this constrained setup, our AARs tried to hack the metric: e.g. one skipped the weak teacher entirely after noticing the most common answer was usually right. We caught these, but it's a warning for future AARs whose hacks may be harder to catch.

xaiItems.author: Anthropic
xaiItems.text: We've raised $65 billion in Series H funding at a $965 billion post-money valuation, led by @AltimeterCap, Dragoneer, @Greenoaks, and @sequoia.
xaiItems.insight: Anthropic achieves unicorn-scale capital at record valuation, dwarfing prior rounds.
xaiItems.impact: Strategic: funds massive compute expansion and research acceleration to close gap with OpenAI/Google.

xaiItems.author: Anthropic
xaiItems.text: Earlier this month, our run-rate revenue crossed $47 billion.
xaiItems.insight: Revenue run-rate signals rapid enterprise adoption of Claude across industries.
xaiItems.impact: Tactical: validates product-market fit and supports high burn rate from new funding.

xaiItems.author: Claude
xaiItems.text: Claude Opus 4.8 is available today on the web, the Claude Platform, and all major cloud platforms.
xaiItems.insight: New flagship model release with dynamic workflows and fast mode at 2.5x speed / 3x cheaper.
xaiItems.impact: Strategic: directly challenges GPT-4o / Gemini 2.5 on coding/agentic tasks.

xaiItems.author: Andrej Karpathy
xaiItems.text: Personal update: I've joined Anthropic. I think the next few years at the frontier of LLMs will be especially formative.
xaiItems.insight: High-profile researcher move strengthens Anthropic's technical bench.
xaiItems.impact: Strategic: signals talent war escalation; boosts credibility in education/R&D community.

xaiItems.author: OpenAI
xaiItems.text: Computer use now works on Windows, so Codex can take action on your Windows computer.
xaiItems.insight: Expands Codex reach beyond macOS to dominant enterprise OS.
xaiItems.impact: tactical: accelerates Windows developer adoption and mobile steering use-cases

xaiItems.author: OpenAI
xaiItems.text: Launching Rosalind Biodefense and expanding trusted access to GPT-Rosalind for U.S. government and allied partners.
xaiItems.insight: Positions OpenAI as direct supplier of frontier models to national biodefense programs.
xaiItems.impact: strategic: deepens government relationships and regulatory goodwill on safety

xaiItems.author: OpenAI Developers
xaiItems.text: Codex background agents now have stable pixel identicons for easier recognition across tabs and transcripts.
xaiItems.insight: Small UX polish that reduces friction for multi-agent workflows.
xaiItems.impact: tactical: improves daily developer productivity and agent orchestration

xaiItems.author: Sam Altman
xaiItems.text: The OpenAI Foundation is making an initial $250M commitment to measurement, transition support, and new approaches to broadly shared prosperity.
xaiItems.insight: Signals major philanthropic pivot toward AI economic impact mitigation.
xaiItems.impact: Positions OpenAI as leader in responsible AI deployment narrative ahead of competitors.

xaiItems.author: Greg Brockman
xaiItems.text: Codex can now create threads, search them, organize them, pin the important ones, and spin up worktrees for parallel tasks.
xaiItems.insight: Demonstrates Codex evolving into autonomous agentic system.
xaiItems.impact: Accelerates developer adoption by reducing manual workflow overhead.

xaiItems.author: OpenAI Newsroom
xaiItems.text: Illinois just passed one of the strongest frontier AI safety laws; OpenAI endorsed SB 315.
xaiItems.insight: OpenAI actively shaping state-level regulatory frameworks.
xaiItems.impact: Creates de facto national safety standard favorable to established players.

xaiItems.author: ChatGPT
xaiItems.text: Table of contents now available for chats with 5+ responses.
xaiItems.insight: Improves long-context usability in consumer product.
xaiItems.impact: Increases stickiness for power users migrating from other tools.

xaiItems.author: Tibo
xaiItems.text: Codex dashboard number made me happy; more news soon.
xaiItems.insight: Teases upcoming Codex usage or performance milestone.
xaiItems.impact: Builds anticipation and signals strong internal momentum.

xaiItems.author: Romain Huet
xaiItems.text: Computer use now available on Windows for Codex.
xaiItems.insight: Extends agentic capabilities to majority desktop OS.
xaiItems.impact: Broadens addressable market for Codex beyond macOS users.

xaiItems.author: Alexander Embiricos
xaiItems.text: Codex now supports more of the Windows developer loop. With Computer use on Windows, Codex can test apps, debug flows, and review work where your project context lives.
xaiItems.insight: Direct expansion of Codex agent capabilities to Windows ecosystem.
xaiItems.impact: Broadens Codex adoption beyond macOS/Linux users; competitive edge vs. Windows-native tools.

xaiItems.author: Guinness Chen
xaiItems.text: Codex can now create threads, search them, organize them, pin the important ones, and spin up worktrees for parallel tasks.
xaiItems.insight: Agentic self-management of its own workflows and context.
xaiItems.impact: Reduces user overhead in long-running Codex sessions; scales multi-task agent use.

xaiItems.author: Gav Verma
xaiItems.text: Plugins are now easy to share across teams! Generate a share URL and drop it into Slack or Teams to distribute custom plugins.
xaiItems.insight: Team-level plugin distribution and reuse now live for Business users.
xaiItems.impact: Accelerates enterprise adoption and internal tool sharing within Codex.

xaiItems.author: Brent Traut
xaiItems.text: Big release coming soon for Windows users!
xaiItems.insight: Internal confirmation of imminent Windows feature drop aligning with public Codex announcements.
xaiItems.impact: Signals coordinated launch; watch for follow-up rollout metrics.

xaiItems.author: Yufeng Zhang
xaiItems.text: My favorite feature! (reply to Codex announcement)
xaiItems.insight: OpenAI researcher endorses new Codex capability
xaiItems.impact: Signals internal excitement around agentic coding tools

xaiItems.author: jason
xaiItems.text: Out inference team is cooking
xaiItems.insight: OpenAI inference team actively iterating
xaiItems.impact: Tactical signal of ongoing model optimization work

xaiItems.author: Peter Steinberger
xaiItems.text: Hackable install if you want it rn, later today in the beta, end of the week on stable. We are not getting any heads up from Anthropic like other partners
xaiItems.insight: OpenAI partner integration delays vs Anthropic
xaiItems.impact: Strategic implication for competitive rollout timing

claudeTeam.name: DARIO
claudeTeam.lore: Anthropic CEO. Anchors the Claude side around frontier-model safety, governance, and strategic clarity.

dossier.tagline: Safety-first macro player for Team Claude.

background.text: Anthropic describes its company mission around reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems; Dario Amodei is the executive face of that safety-first posture.

keyProjects.name: Anthropic safety and governance frame
keyProjects.description: The Claude-side narrative is built on Anthropic's public positioning around responsible frontier AI.

strengths.label: Strategic framing
strengths.description: Turns model competition into a governance and safety argument rather than a pure feature race.

narrative.title: Defensive Supercycle
narrative.text: A boss character built around patience, policy terrain, and long-horizon scaling discipline.

claudeTeam.name: DANIELA
claudeTeam.lore: Anthropic president. Public company materials put her close to product and operating structure decisions.

dossier.tagline: The operating spine behind Claude's enterprise posture.

background.text: Anthropic's company materials place Daniela Amodei in the public leadership layer of the organization.

keyProjects.name: Anthropic operating model
keyProjects.description: A leadership role tied to converting frontier research into a durable company and customer platform.

strengths.label: Org translation
strengths.description: Connects the lab's safety thesis to the operating structure needed for enterprise adoption.

narrative.title: Org Velocity
narrative.text: A support-class fighter whose buffs come from coordination, process, and keeping the lab's field position coherent.

claudeTeam.name: MIKE
claudeTeam.lore: Anthropic Labs builder. Anthropic says he moved from CPO into Labs to build experimental Claude products with Ben Mann.

dossier.tagline: Product experimenter turning Claude capability into new surfaces.

background.text: Anthropic's Labs announcement says Mike Krieger moved into Labs with Ben Mann to incubate experimental products at the frontier of Claude capabilities.

keyProjects.name: Anthropic Labs
keyProjects.description: A product incubation group for frontier Claude products, including experimental workflow surfaces.

strengths.label: Prototype-to-product loop
strengths.description: Works at the boundary between research capability and product experience.

narrative.title: Experiment Loop
narrative.text: A fast builder class that wins by finding the product shape before the rest of the field names the category.

keyProjects.name: Public AI education

strengths.label: Frontier credibility
strengths.description: Carries rare status across research, industry, and public education.

narrative.title: The Transfer Window Shock
narrative.text: A late-season unlock for Team Claude: research prestige, educator aura, and a pre-training questline aimed straight at the model core.

codexTeam.name: JASON
codexTeam.lore: Developer Experience Engineer on the Codex team at OpenAI. Creator of Instructor, a Python library for structured LLM outputs.

dossier.tagline: Structured-output specialist bringing schema discipline to Codex.

background.text: Jason Liu says he is a Developer Experience Engineer on the Codex team at OpenAI.
His site says he created Instructor, a Python library for structured LLM outputs that OpenAI cited as inspiration for structured outputs.

keyProjects.name: Instructor
keyProjects.description: Instructor maps LLM outputs to Pydantic models through a response_model workflow and keeps close compatibility with the OpenAI Python SDK.

keyProjects.name: Structured outputs education
keyProjects.description: Weights & Biases lists Jason as the instructor for a course on structured outputs and data validation.

strengths.label: Schema-first product sense
strengths.description: Makes LLM behavior legible to developers by turning freeform text into typed, validated outputs.

narrative.title: Pydantic Lock
narrative.text: A control-class fighter who wins by forcing messy model output into typed lanes before the next combo starts.

keyProjects.name: OpenClaw
keyProjects.description: A viral open-source personal-agent project that OpenAI said would live in a foundation while Steinberger worked on personal agents.

scoringRubric.disclaimer: Arcade scores are narrative indices, not scientific benchmarks. Each number should point to a public-source rationale.

characterStats.ATK: Product or research offensive pressure: launches, model strength, public agenda-setting, or concrete builder leverage.
characterStats.DEF: Safety, trust, compliance, security, governance, and ability to absorb scrutiny.
characterStats.SPD: Shipping speed and signal velocity: how quickly the person or surface turns capability into visible motion.
characterStats.COMBO: Platform leverage: integrations, ecosystem effects, workflow chaining, and ability to compound with other moves.
characterStats.HP: Durability: capital, compute, org depth, adoption, technical moat, and staying power.

teamPower.momentum: Recent public signals plus strategic weight of launches, hires, partnerships, and adoption proof.
teamPower.mindshare: Share of attention in developer, enterprise, and public product narratives.
teamPower.enterprise: Evidence of paid business workflows, regulated-industry adoption, partnerships, and deployment credibility.
teamPower.ship: Visible product releases, workflow surface expansion, and cadence of concrete shipping moves.

scoringRubric.verticals: Vertical conquest blends public product fit, enterprise proof, partner distribution, and inferred adoption pressure. Close scores are intentionally contested.
